docs.title: Ingestion Worker Runbook
docs.content:
Ingestion Worker Runbook

This page documents the ingestion worker pipeline and its key functions.

## Claim loop
The main claim loop is implemented in `Worker._claim_one` in services/ingestion/worker.py. It uses PostgreSQL's FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED to safely claim queue rows without contention.

## Dead-letter handling
When a row exceeds MAX_ATTEMPTS, `Worker._move_to_dlq` parks it in the DLQ table. This function is the canonical place to add DLQ alerting.

## On-call notes
If the DLQ grows unexpectedly, check for schema mismatches in the normalizer output — they are the #1 cause of DLQ spikes.

docs.title: services/ingestion/worker.py
docs.content:
# services/ingestion/worker.py

class Worker:
    async def _claim_one(self) -> QueueRow | None:
        """Claim one pending queue row using FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED."""
        ...

    async def _move_to_dlq(self, row: QueueRow, reason: str) -> None:
        """Park a row in the dead-letter queue after max attempts."""
        ...

expected_edges.note: Notion runbook explicitly documents the Worker class in worker.py, naming its key functions and their purpose
